podcasts.title: City Arts & Lectures
podcasts.author: City Arts & Lectures
podcasts.reviewText: The quality of this podcast is entirely the guests. I do really like the Stephen Breyer episodes

podcasts.title: Very Bad Wizards
podcasts.author: Tamler Sommers & David Pizarro
podcasts.reviewText: I was recommended this by a teacher in High School. I found the hosts annoying and I learned nothing.

podcasts.title: SciShow Tangents
podcasts.author: Complexly
podcasts.reviewText: I think having 2 incorrect facts said very confidently every episode is a bad for learning.

podcasts.title: Conversations with People Who Hate Me
podcasts.author: Dylan Marron & TED

podcasts.title: The Modi Raj from The Economist
podcasts.author: The Economist
podcasts.reviewText: This helped me understand India much better. I liked this more than the similar The Prince.

podcasts.title: The Edge of Sentience by Jonathan Birch
podcasts.author: Aaron Bergman

podcasts.title: LGBT Bar NY Podcast
podcasts.author: LeGaL - LGBT Bar of New York

podcasts.title: Editor's Picks from The Economist
podcasts.author: The Economist
podcasts.reviewText: 20% are good picks. Not much here that isn't covered by other Economists podcasts.

podcasts.title: Hello, Puzzlers!
podcasts.author: Neuhaus Ideas
podcasts.reviewText: You know the friend who orders a sandwich but removes all the ingredients except bread and protein? They seem to savor their meal, but are seemingly missing its major components. This podcast is the sandwich. The ads, the fluff, the guest introductions crucially must be skipped. Then it's great.

podcasts.title: The Weekend Intelligence from The Economist
podcasts.author: The Economist

podcasts.title: Money Stuff: The Podcast
podcasts.author: Bloomberg
podcasts.reviewText: The hosts seem smart but I hate their voice and how they talk. Maybe read them instead. I have learned some from this podcast but this podcast was ultimately unhelpful since it's executive purpose isn't education.

podcasts.title: Risky Business with Nate Silver and Maria Konnikova
podcasts.author: Pushkin Industries
podcasts.reviewText: I'd read Nate Silver instead. I don't think he comes across clearly on this podcast. Valuable to listen 3 months before a US election perhaps.

podcasts.title: Conversations with Tyler
podcasts.author: Mercatus Center at George Mason University

podcasts.title: The Marginal Revolution Podcast
podcasts.author: Mercatus Center at George Mason University

podcasts.title: The Economics Show
podcasts.author: Financial Times
podcasts.reviewText: In some other econ podcasts, I feel like the hosts are lacking with their explanations and lazy with their format. Chad Jones and Soumaya Keynes are not. They are great and I've learned a lot.

podcasts.title: The Animal Law Podcast
podcasts.author: Mariann Sullivan, Law Professor, Pundit, Vegan

podcasts.title: A Way with Words - language, linguistics, and callers from all over
podcasts.author: Martha Barnette and Grant Barrett. Produced by Stefanie Levine.
podcasts.reviewText: I like this! I would love for the producer to be more ambitious and have more of everything: most games, having online questions answered, more hosts for multi-day coverage, ... . I usually am not usually compelled to listen when this comes out but like it every time I do listen.

favoriteEpisodes.title: Why should ethical anti-realists do ethics?

podcasts.title: How I Learned to Love Shrimp
podcasts.author: James Özden

podcasts.title: Clearer Thinking with Spencer Greenberg
podcasts.author: Spencer Greenberg
podcasts.reviewText: I think this is a great podcast, especially for those in need of thinking more clearly. I find the format to now be repetitive and want deeper exploration of the topics. I think the clearer thinking is clear, but somewhat bland and abstract. This does have a place in my heart as one of the good early podcasts I listened to.

podcasts.title: Bio(un)ethical
podcasts.author: with Leah Pierson and Sophie Gibert

podcasts.title: The Ezra Klein Show
podcasts.author: New York Times Opinion
podcasts.reviewText: A podcast with many words and tones and thoughts. I do think Ezra and his guests, especially politicians, end up speaking a lot but saying very little. However, this podcast has helped me when I've struggle to think about difficult political topics; aided by Ezra's detachment.

podcasts.title: On Being with Krista Tippett
podcasts.author: On Being Studios
podcasts.reviewText: This podcast feels like Joe Rogan if he was very kind and calm.

podcasts.title: The New Yorker Radio Hour
podcasts.author: WNYC Studios and The New Yorker

podcasts.title: The New Yorker: The Writer's Voice - New Fiction from The New Yorker
podcasts.author: The New Yorker

podcasts.title: 80,000 Hours Podcast
podcasts.author: Rob, Luisa, and the 80000 Hours team

favoriteEpisodes.title: Rebuilding after apocalypse: What 13 experts say about bouncing back
